title: Update on the epidemiological typing methods for Acinetobacter baumannii.
author_string: Rafei, Rayane, Osman, Marwan, Dabboussi, Fouad, Hamze, Monzer
abstract: The outstanding ability of Acinetobacter baumannii to cause outbreaks and acquire multidrug resistance motivated the development of a plethora of typing techniques, which can help infection preventionists and hospital epidemiologists to more efficiently implement intervention controls. Nowadays, the world is witnessing a gradual transition from traditional typing methodology to whole genome sequencing-based approaches. Such approaches are opening new prospects and applications never achieved by existing typing methods. Herein, we provide the reader with an updated review on A. baumannii typing methods recapping the added value of well-established techniques previously applied for A. baumannii and detailing new ones (as clustered regularly interspaced short palindromic repeats-based typing) with a special focus on whole genome sequencing.
